note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/Dioph.lean:665; module docstring is explicitly framed around Hilbert's tenth problem and Matiyasevic's theorem, and pow_dioph proves the power function is Diophantine (Matiyasevic's key lemma, the last piece of the classical undecidability proof). File's own TODO (line 47) says 'Finish the solution of Hilbert's tenth problem' — the final undecidability statement itself is NOT yet formalized, only this DPRM-theorem supporting machinery.

Mathlib/Order/OrderIsoNat.lean:177-179, docstring: 'This is the infinitary Erdős–Szekeres theorem, and an important lemma in the usual proof of Bolzano-Weierstrass for ℝ.' This is the infinite-sequence analogue, not the classical finite quantitative theorem (sequence length > (r-1)(s-1) forces a monotone subsequence of length r or s) that mathworld:Erdos-SzekeresTheorem describes.

Mathlib/Topology/TietzeExtension.lean:76, proves extension along a closed embedding for any target with a `TietzeExtension` class instance (ℝ, ℝ×ℝ, ℂ, ι→ℝ, ℝ≥0, etc, per file docstring lines 26-29). This is the classical Tietze extension theorem generalized to a class of targets — a special case of Dugundji's theorem (general locally convex spaces, with a convex-hull-preserving image), which has no convexity-preserving statement in Mathlib.

Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/DifferentialForm/Basic.lean:259-266, 'Exterior derivative commutes with pullback': proves d(f*ω) = f*(dω) where the pullback of the form ω along f is written inline as `fun x ↦ (ω (f x)).compContinuousLinearMap (fderiv 𝕜 f x)`. Covers differential forms on normed spaces only — file TODO (line 37) says manifold-level forms are 'not defined yet' — and there is no standalone named `pullback` definition, only this inline expression built from `ContinuousAlternatingMap.compContinuousLinearMap`.
